steps.title: Use corresponding angles
steps.content: The triangles are similar. Since $J$ corresponds to $M$ and $L$ and $P$ are both right angles, the remaining acute angles $K$ and $N$ correspond, so $\cos N=\cos K$.

steps.title: Identify corresponding angles in the similar triangles
steps.content: Triangle JKL ~ Triangle MNP with angle J corresponding to angle M. Since angles L and P are both right angles (90°), angles K and N are the two acute angles in their respective triangles.

steps.title: Use complementary angles in right triangles
steps.content:
In a right triangle, the two acute angles are complementary:
$$J + K = 90^{\circ}$$
$$M + N = 90^{\circ}$$
Since $J = M$ (corresponding angles), we have $N = 90^{\circ} - M = 90^{\circ} - J = K$.

steps.title: Find cos N
steps.content: Since $N = K$ and $\cos K = \frac{1}{18}$, we have $\cos N = \frac{1}{18}$, which is choice A.
